title: Crepidopteris gracillima
taxonomy: Crepidopteris gracillima is classified within the family Matoniaceae. It belongs to the order Cyatheales, which is a group of ancient, woody ferns. This species is further categorized as a genus within the family Matoniaceae. Crepidopteris gracillima's classification is part of the broader plant kingdom, specifically within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises ferns and their allies. The specific classification of Crepidopteris gracillima can be summarized as: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Cyatheales, Family: Matoniaceae.
uses: Crepidopteris gracillima, also known as the slender maidenhair fern, is a plant with a long history of traditional use in various cultures. It is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever and digestive issues. In some Asian cultures, it is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Crepidopteris gracillima for medicinal purposes.
